folio.label: 62r
folio.l: Swiche maner necessaries , as ben plesynges
To folk , that han I wedded hem , witú rynges
And ley a lite , here holynesse a syde
As for the tyme , it may no bet betide
¢ On hire he gat , a knaue child a noone
And to a Busshop~ , and his Constable eke
He tok his wyf to kepe , whan he is gone
To Scotlondward , his foomen for to seke
Now fair Custaunce , æ is so humble & meke
So longe is goon w childe , to that stille
She halt hire chaumbre , a bidynge céstes wylle
¢ The tyme is come , a knaue child she beer
Mauricius at the fontstone , they hi~ calle
This Constable , dotú fortú come , a messanger
And wroot vn to his kynge , æ cleped was Alle
How that this blisful tidynge , is byfalle
And othere tidynggã , spedeful for to seye
He hatú the &lbar;re , and fortú he gotú his weye

note.notetxt: 630there is a cross in dry point next to this stanza.ODR

folio.l: ¢ This messanger , to doon hise auauntage
Vn to the kynges moder , ridetú swithe
And saluetú hire faire , in his langage
Madame q¶ he , ©e may be glad and blithe
And thanketú god , an hundred thou©sand sithe
My lady Queene hatú child , w oute doute
To ioye & blisse , to al this regne a boute
¢ Lo heere the &lbar;res , seeled of this thynge
That I moot beere , witú al the hast I may
If ©e wol augút´ vn to ©oure sone the kynge
I am ©oure seruau~t , bothe nygút and day
Donegilde answered , as now at this tyme , nay
But heere al nygút , I wol thow take thy reest
To morwe wol I sey thee , what me leest
¢ This messanger drank sadly , Ale and wyne
And stolen were hise &lbar;res , Preuyly
Out of his box , while he slepe , as a swyne
And countrefeted was , ful subtilly
A nother &lbar;re , wrougút ful synfully
Vn to the kynge , directe of this mateer
Fro his Constable , as ©e shuln after heer
¢ The &lbar;re spak , the Queene delyuered was
Of so horrible , a feendliche creature
That in the Castel , noon so hardy was
That any while , durst there endure
The moder was an Elf , be auenture
I comen be charmes , or by sorcerie
